name: climatebench

data_origin.blend: NorESM2 climate-model simulations under various forcing scenarios

env_axis.name: scenario
env_axis.shift_mechanism: held-out SSP245 (mid-mitigation regime); train on others

facts: NorESM2 simulations under multiple SSP + idealized forcing scenarios; held-out SSP245 standard OOD test.
hypotheses: Held-out SSP245 reveals forced-response generalization (the policy-relevant target), rather than letting methods exploit historical-baseline information shared across scenarios.
SSP245 is a curator-chosen representative of mid-mitigation scenarios rather than a naturally occurring regime.
intuitions: ClimateBench v2 or extensions likely exist in the 2024-25 literature.
Cross-CMIP6-model emulation benchmarks probably exist, and method ordering likely transfers NorESM2↔UKESM1 only partially.
Climate foundation models such as ClimaX and Aurora likely have documented behavior on SSP scenarios.
unknowns: Whether climate-emulator generalization (NorESM2-to-NorESM2 cross-scenario) maps to climate-prediction generalization (any-model-to-real-Earth) cannot be determined here — these are categorically different shifts that the literature often conflates.
The SSP scenario tree is itself CMIP6's curatorial choice; held-out SSP245 represents an unseen specific scenario, not an unseen real future.
epistemic_state.boundary: Simulator-derived data: useful for testing method behavior on climate-like data, not for evidencing real-world climate predictions.

materialization_status: train_val.tar.gz (839 MB) + CMIP6.zip (1473 MB) + test.tar.gz (74 MB) — total 2.3 GB from Zenodo record 5196512.
